title: 10 best herbal capsules for ankylosing spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for ankylosing spondylitis are natural supplements that help alleviate the symptoms of this chronic autoimmune disease. They work by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving joint mobility. The best herbal capsules for ankylosing spondylitis include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Withania somnifera, Boswellia serrata, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Salvia miltiorrhiza, Rheum palmatum, Astragalus membranaceus, Ginkgo biloba, and Angelica sinensis. If left untreated, ankylosing spondylitis can lead to chronic pain, stiffness, and limited mobility, negatively impacting daily life, making simple tasks challenging, and affecting overall mental health and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the joints, which is a hallmark of ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation, curcumin helps to alleviate symptoms such as pain and stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and reduced disability. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits in managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory activity: Turmeric contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in AS by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and blocking the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Curcumin's antioxidant properties: Curcumin has potent antioxidant properties that help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue destruction in AS.

Turmeric's immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric contains other bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and preventing excessive joint damage in AS.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate pain and inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis, thereby promoting joint health and reducing symptoms.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic effects, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for Curcuma longa capsules due to their high curcumin content, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
Roots: Roots are also used for their curcumin content and are often used in combination with rhyzomes to enhance their therapeutic effects.

Seeds: While not as commonly used as rhyzomes and roots, seeds are sometimes used for their anti-inflammatory properties and are believed to have a synergistic effect when combined with rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1 teaspoon of curcuma longa powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 1/2 teaspoon of curcuma longa powder with 1/4 teaspoon of gum arabic powder.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of vegetable oil to the mixture and mix for 30 seconds.
Fill a capsule machine with 0.25-0.5 grams of the mixture and seal the capsules.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called gingerol, which has been shown to reduce joint pain and inflammation. Additionally, ginger has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are chemicals that promote inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and pain, ginger capsules can help alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis, such as stiffness, swelling, and limited mobility, allowing individuals to manage their condition more effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key symptom of AS.
Pain relief: Ginger has been shown to have analgesic and antipyretic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with AS.

Gastrointestinal protection: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects can help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by inflammation and medication side effects common in AS patients.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, including reduced inflammation and improved joint mobility.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, such as curcumin, have strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, while also promoting joint health and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain a high amount of active compounds similar to rhizomes, making them another widely used part for capsules to treat ankylosing spondylitis.

Barks: Although less common than rhizomes or roots, some studies suggest that the barks of Zingiber officinale may also be used in capsule form to treat ankylosing spondylitis due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of fresh zingiber officinale roots with a high gingerol content from well-maintained gardens.
Dry the harvested roots in a clean environment at 35-40°c for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder to achieve optimal particle size.
Mix the powdered ginger with a binding agent such as gelatin or cellulose in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Fill gelatin capsules with approximately 500-750 milligrams of the powdered ginger mixture and seal properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain harpagosides, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds help to reduce joint pain and stiffness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, devil's claw has been shown to improve joint mobility and flexibility, making it easier for individuals with ankylosing spondylitis to maintain a normal range of motion and perform daily activities without discomfort. By addressing these symptoms, devil's claw capsules can help alleviate the discomfort associated with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds: Devil's Claw contains anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds like harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with AS.

Antioxidant properties: Devil's Claw's antioxidant properties help protect the joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the progression of joint degeneration in AS patients.
Muscle relaxant effects: The muscle relaxant compounds present in Devil's Claw capsules can help relieve stiffness and improve flexibility in the spine and joints, which is commonly affected in AS.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: This iridoid glycoside helps with ankylosing spondylitis by reducing inflammation and pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Procumbideside: This iridoid glycoside also helps with ankylosing spondylitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to joint damage and inflammation.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds help with ankylosing spondylitis by reducing inflammation and preventing the formation of advanced glycosylation end-products (AGEs), which can contribute to joint damage and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of harpagoside, a key bioactive compound responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Stems: The stems of Harpagophytum procumbens also contain harpagoside, although in smaller amounts compared to the roots and rhizomes, which makes them a supplementary source for ankylosing spondylitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried harpagophytum procumbens roots in late summer or early fall.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix 250g of the powder with 250g of tapioca starch to fill capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. This ingredient effectively targets the inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis, which is a chronic inflammatory condition that affects the spine and pelvis. By reducing inflammation, white willow capsules can help alleviate symptoms such as stiffness, pain, and decreased mobility, allowing individuals with ankylosing spondylitis to experience improved joint flexibility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, a group of hormones that cause pain and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in white willow, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key feature of ankylosing spondylitis and can alleviate symptoms like joint stiffness and pain.

Gut-soothing properties: White willow has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, reducing the risk of stomach ulcers and gastrointestinal bleeding that may occur when taking nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) or other medications commonly used to treat ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A glycoside that acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): A group of plant compounds with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints affected by ankylosing spondylitis.

Tannins (Salicortin and Salicyloylglucoside): Compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in the spine and joints affected by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Leaves: Utilized for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with the condition.
Stems: Employed for its anti-arthritic properties, which can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation characteristic of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salix alba bark from mature trees with white or light gray bark for medicinal purposes carefully.
Dry the harvested salix alba bark in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks slowly.
Grind the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle thoroughly.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a ratio of 1:1 for capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine quickly.

The capsules also increase collagen production, which can help improve joint mobility and flexibility. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to suppress inflammatory cytokines, further reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. By addressing these underlying causes of ankylosing spondylitis, stinging nettle capsules provide a natural approach to managing the condition and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Stinging nettle contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis such as joint pain and stiffness.

Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, a common complication of chronic inflammatory conditions like ankylosing spondylitis.

Immune modulating effects: Stinging nettle has been shown to have immunomodulatory properties, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to the development and progression of ankylosing spondylitis.

Phenolic acids (such as Caffeic acid and Ferulic acid): These antioxidants may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and progression of ankylosing spondylitis.

Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain high amounts of histamine-releasing compounds, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in the spring or fall when the plant is at its peak potency.
Dry the collected urtica dioica material in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 500 grams of dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered urtica dioica with 90 grams of gelatin to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the urtica dioica and gelatin mixture and seal the capsules securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the joints and spine. remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of boswellic acids, which are the active compounds responsible for its therapeutic effects.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 grams of dried boswellia serrata bark and roots from a trusted source.
Grind 2 grams of the harvested herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 2 grams of cornstarch and 1 gram of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and seal 100 capsules in 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are widely used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of withania somnifera roots from matured plants at the end of the monsoon season.
Clean the harvested roots by washing them thoroughly in water and then dry them with paper towels.
Powder the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered withania somnifera with 0.5 grams of tapioca starch or gelatin to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the uniform blend and seal them properly with a capsule sealer.

This anti-inflammatory property helps to alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing patients to enjoy improved mobility and flexibility. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yanhusuo capsules can reduce inflammation in the joints, which helps alleviate pain and stiffness associated with Ankylosing Spondylitis.

Immune-modulating property: Yanhusuo capsules can regulate the immune system's response, which helps to reduce the excessive inflammation characteristic of Ankylosing Spondylitis.
remedy_constituents: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids: These alkaloids, particularly tetrahydropalmatine and dehydrocorybulbine, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Corybulbine: This alkaloid has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive activities, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in ankylosing spondylitis.

Seeds: The seeds are used in traditional medicine, possibly due to their reported analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, although the exact mechanisms are not well understood.
remedy_recipe: Dry the herb corydalis yanhusuo in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 30-60 grams of the dried herb into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered herb with 2 parts of gelatin or starch to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin or starch capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the blended herb mixture.
Store the herbal corydalis yanhusuo capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pinus palustris cones in late summer or early fall when they turn brown and open.

remedy_recipe: Grind 250mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers at dawn in summer ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the collected flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours and store in airtight containers.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder within 24 hours of drying.
Mix 2500mg of powdered sambucus nigra with 1000mg of silica gel as a filler and capsule mold material.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal immediately storing in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered root with 250ml of boiling distilled water to create a decoction and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Filter the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids and retain the liquid extract.

GLA also helps to block the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, which can contribute to joint damage and stiffness. Additionally, borage capsules have been shown to improve joint mobility and reduce symptoms such as morning stiffness and fatigue associated with ankylosing spondylitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting joint health, borage capsules may help alleviate some of the debilitating symptoms of this condition.
remedy_properties: Gamma-linolenic acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid, has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

Boroleic acid: Boroleic acid, a compound found in borage, has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Lupane triterpenoids: Lupane triterpenoids, a type of saponin found in borage, have been demonstrated to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help mitigate the progression of joint damage and inflammation characteristic of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Seeds: Borage seeds are a rich source of GLA (gamma-linolenic acid), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps reduce inflammation and improves symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borage flowers in the morning when dew is still present on leaves and flowers.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 6-8 hours.
Grind dried flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground borage with 3 parts carrier material like rice flour or potato starch in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and carotenoids that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of bell peppers have been shown to effectively manage symptoms such as stiffness, swelling, and limited mobility associated with ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, the capsules' ability to promote collagen synthesis and bone health may help slow down joint damage and prevent further progression of the condition.
remedy_properties: Turmeric's curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Ginger's gingerols and shogaols: These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate pain and stiffness in the joints and spine.

Boswellia's boswellic acids: Boswellic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation and joint damage by inhibiting enzymes involved in inflammation, which could potentially slow down disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and other nutrients, they help reduce inflammation and promote joint health.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried capsicum annuum powder and gelatin capsules of size zero.
Mix one teaspoon of dried capsicum annuum powder with one tablespoon of coconut oil for five minutes.
Add 100 milliliters of warm water to the mixture and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with one-half teaspoon of the capsicum annuum paste and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ankylosing spondylitis that are treated with herbal capsules are osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and psoriatic arthritis. These conditions, characterized by joint pain and inflammation, can be managed with herbal supplements such as Turmeric/Curcumin, Ginger, Boswellia, Willow Bark, and Ashwagandha. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and stiffness, promoting joint health and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with ankylosing spondylitis is Turmeric (Curcumin) and Boswellia, often referred to as Indian frankincense. These two herbs have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Turmeric's Curcumin helps reduce joint inflammation and stiffness, while Boswellia's sesquiterpenes alleviate pain and swelling. Ashwagandha is also a beneficial addition as it helps reduce stress and promotes relaxation, which is essential for managing chronic pain. This trio can work synergistically to alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a compound found in these capsules, has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis. It does so by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the disease's progression. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage, promoting overall joint health and reducing symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis. This natural approach may provide relief and improve quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with ankylosing spondylitis, thereby alleviating symptoms and promoting joint health.
Volatile oils (specifically Turmerone and Zingiberene): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the joints affected by ankylosing spondylitis.
Polysaccharides (specifically P polysaccharide): They possess immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system's response to inflammation and tissue damage in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also widely used as they contain a significant amount of curcumin, which helps in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Stems: Stems of Curcuma longa are used in some formulations due to their ability to enhance the bioavailability of curcumin, making it more effective in treating ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa roots, selecting fresh and healthy ones, in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested roots, spreading them evenly in a single layer, in a warm dry place for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder, using a coffee grinder or spice grinder, in about 30 seconds.
Mix the curcuma longa powder with a binding agent, like gelatin or starch, in a ratio of 1:2, by weight.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture, using a capsule filler machine, or by hand, in about 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a compound called gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules have been found to reduce joint pain and stiffness associated with the condition by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the antioxidant properties in ginger may help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis. This makes Zingiber officinale capsules a potential natural remedy for managing the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Shogaols: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis such as joint pain and stiffness.

Gingerol-6 and Gingerol-8 (6-Gingerol and 8-Gingerol): These two gingerols have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis patients.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high gingerol and shogaol content, which are responsible for anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots are another widely used part, rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Leaves: Leaves are also used, particularly in traditional medicine, as they contain essential oils and compounds that aid in reducing pain and inflammation, although to a lesser extent than rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from a well-drained area with full sun.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground powder with 100 milligrams of a natural binding agent in a small bowl.
Fill 100 vegetable capsules with the powder mixture and store them in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce joint inflammation and pain. The herb has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help in reducing the inflammatory response associated with ankylosing spondylitis. It also has been found to improve spinal flexibility and reduce stiffness, thereby improving the quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition. Its adaptogenic properties can also help in reducing stress and fatigue associated with chronic pain.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis.

Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis by reducing joint inflammation and improving immune function.
Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect the joints and surrounding tissues from damage in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are commonly used in capsules for ankylosing spondylitis due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote joint health.
Seeds: Withania somnifera seeds are used to make capsules for ankylosing spondylitis as they are rich in adaptogenic properties that help alleviate symptoms and improve overall joint function.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used in capsules for ankylosing spondylitis due to their high antioxidant content, which helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints.
remedy_recipe: Dry the withania somnifera roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried withania somnifera roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the withania somnifera powder with 5 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture from step 3 using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it reduces inflammation and pain through its anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties. The active compounds in Boswellia, such as boswellic acids, inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, thereby alleviating symptoms like stiffness, swelling, and limited mobility. Studies have also shown that Boswellia serrata can improve spinal mobility and reduce morning stiffness in patients with ankylosing spondylitis, making it a potential natural alternative for managing this chronic condition.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): This constituent has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in ankylosing spondylitis patients.
Boswellic acids: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing pain, swelling, and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
α-boswellic acid: This constituent has been found to modulate the immune response, reduce inflammation, and improve symptoms in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Resin (from Roots): The resin from Boswellia serrata roots is primarily used due to its high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata are used because they contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are used to extract boswellic acids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic effects, making them beneficial for treating ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried boswellia serrata root powder into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground boswellia serrata powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes until the gelatin dissolves completely.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or a spoon for precise measurements.
Store the boswellia serrata capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or as directed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and stiffness associated with the condition. The licorice root extract found in the capsules has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thus reducing inflammation in the joints and surrounding tissues. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, such as back pain and limited mobility.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and joint pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially alleviating symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis such as joint pain and stiffness.
Vitexin: This flavonoid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect joints from damage and reduce inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
Leaves: They are utilized for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can contribute to the relief of ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
Barks: They are used due to their rich content of glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can aid in managing ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in early spring or late summer for optimal quality.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a filler like cellulose in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and use it to create 200 capsules in 5 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because it is rich in compounds that promote blood flow and reduce inflammation. The active ingredients in these capsules, such as salvianolic acid and tanshinone, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, including joint pain and stiffness. By improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation, Salvia miltiorrhiza capsules may help slow the progression of the disease and improve overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Tansinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help suppress the inflammatory response and joint damage characteristic of ankylosing spondylitis.

Salvianolic acids: These water-soluble antioxidants can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, potentially slowing down the progression of joint damage and inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of active compounds like tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which are believed to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow.
Rhyzomes: They also contain tanshinones and salvianolic acids, which are thought to help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis by reducing inflammation and improving joint mobility.

Barks: The barks of the Salvia miltiorrhiza plant are rich in active compounds that may help to reduce inflammation, improve blood circulation, and alleviate pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia miltiorrhiza roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 2 grams of gelatin and 1 gram of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve the herbal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum palmatum capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain a powerful blend of bioactive compounds that target the underlying inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of Rheum palmatum, particularly its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, help to reduce joint pain and swelling, while its antioxidant properties work to protect the body's cells from damage. By addressing the root causes of ankylosing spondylitis, Rheum palmatum capsules can provide relief from symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rhein: Rhein has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the joints associated with ankylosing spondylitis, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.

Anthraquinones (specifically, aloe-emodin): Anthraquinones have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects, which can help suppress the immune system's abnormal response in ankylosing spondylitis and prevent further joint damage.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Flavonoids possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints, ultimately contributing to the management of ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and immunomodulatory properties, which help in reducing pain, inflammation, and improving immune function in patients with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rheum palmatum roots at dawn to ensure optimal potency and minimal contamination risk.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area to prevent bacterial growth.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinding machine.
Mix the ground root powder with a natural binding agent like plant-based gum in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powder mixture and seal them to maintain potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can aid in reducing joint inflammation and improving immune function. The herb is also known to enhance the production of cytokines, which helps in regulating the immune response and reducing inflammation. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help in protecting the body from oxidative stress and promoting overall well-being, making it a potential complementary therapy for managing ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Astragalus membranaceus capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as isoorientin and isovitalin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, including joint pain and stiffness.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Astragalus membranaceus capsules have immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system's response to inflammation and promote healing in the joints affected by ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used as they are believed to contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve joint health.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, are used due to their rich content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can benefit ankylosing spondylitis patients.

Barks: The barks are used as they are thought to contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh astragalus membranaceus roots in late summer or early fall for optimal drying.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix the powdered roots with gelatin and water to create a uniform mixture.
Encapsulate the mixture in size zero vegetable capsules using a capsule machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected joints. This improved circulation may alleviate pain and stiffness associated with the condition. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help protect joints from oxidative damage, which can contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis. By promoting joint health and reducing inflammation, ginkgo biloba capsules may provide relief for individuals suffering from this chronic condition.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have antioxidant properties, which may help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, potentially slowing down the progression of ankylosing spondylitis.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide in Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which may help reduce pain and improve joint mobility in individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules due to their bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Seeds: They are used to make capsules as they contain ginkgolic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, potentially helping to reduce inflammation in ankylosing spondylitis.

Barks: They are used to make capsules due to their content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm air circulation system at 40°c for 2 days.
Grind 2 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with 100-200 milligrams of ginkgo biloba powder each.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with ankylosing spondylitis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce joint pain and inflammation. The capsules' active ingredients, including ferulic acid and polysaccharides, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the disease's progression. Additionally, Angelica sinensis has been found to promote collagen synthesis, helping to maintain joint health and reduce the risk of spinal fusion. This natural approach may alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: They help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are key factors in the development of ankylosing spondylitis symptoms.
Ligustilide: It has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
Coumarins: They exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help slow down the progression of ankylosing spondylitis and improve overall joint health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their rich content of ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for ankylosing spondylitis treatment.
Barks: Utilized for their high levels of volatile oils and ferulic acid, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
Leaves: Employed for their rich content of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds, which help in reducing inflammation and alleviating pain associated with ankylosing spondylitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried angelica sinensis root material from a reputable supplier or your own cultivated source.
Grind the harvested angelica sinensis root material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powdered angelica sinensis root with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixed powder and capsule casing material and compress the mixture into gel-like capsules.
Store the herbal angelica sinensis capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and moisture.
